documents.title: Phishing email detection
documents.content: The phishing email detection note says analysts confirmed suspicious links by comparing sender reputation, domain alignment, and attachment behavior before opening an incident.

documents.title: Email notification settings
documents.content: The email notification settings guide explains how users choose digest frequency, mute project updates, and route low priority alerts to a weekly summary.

documents.title: SMTP delivery failure
documents.content: The SMTP delivery failure runbook says engineers check message queues, retry status, bounce codes, and DNS records when legitimate email fails to deliver.

documents.title: Security alert triage
documents.content: The security alert triage note says responders classify severity, confirm affected systems, assign an owner, and create an incident only when evidence supports escalation.

queries.query: how did analysts confirm suspicious phishing links
where can users choose digest frequency
what should engineers inspect for delivery failures
when should responders create an incident from an alert
